objective: Define the sensitive keys and non-sensitive variables Atom depends on so trusted workers can explain missing configuration in plain language without storing real secrets in Git.

secrets.name: OPENAI_API_KEY
secrets.ifMissing: OpenAI-powered features will fail or need to be disabled.

secrets.name: GEMINI_API_KEY
secrets.ifMissing: Gemini-powered features will fail or need to be disabled.

secrets.name: JWT_SECRET
secrets.plainEnglish: Protects user sessions and signed tokens.
secrets.ifMissing: Users may not be able to log in safely, or sessions may be insecure.

secrets.name: DATABASE_URL
secrets.plainEnglish: Tells Atom how to connect to the database.
secrets.ifMissing: Anything that needs saved data may fail.

secrets.name: WHATSAPP_VERIFY_TOKEN
secrets.ifMissing: WhatsApp verification or message delivery may fail.

publicVariables.name: EXPO_PORT
publicVariables.plainEnglish: Controls the local Expo web port.

conclusion: Trusted workers should use this manifest as the source of truth, never ask for raw secret values in issues or commits, and always explain missing configuration with choices and consequences.
